title: Cyperus teres
intro: Cyperus teres, commonly known as umbrella sedge or umbrella papyrus, is a perennial aquatic plant native to tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties, offering potential health benefits such as wound healing, fever reduction, and immune system support. The bioactive constituents of Cyperus teres include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Cyperus teres is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues and skin infections.
taxonomy: Cyperus teres is classified within the family Cyperaceae, which is commonly known as the sedge family. This family consists of more than 5,000 species of grass-like plants found worldwide. Within the Cyperaceae family, Cyperus teres is placed in the genus Cyperus, a group of plants commonly known as papyrus sedge or umbrella sedge. The genus Cyperus contains over 700 species of sedge plants, many of which are aquatic or semi-aquatic. Cyperus teres is further categorized into the section Cyperus, which includes about 200 species of sedge plants.
uses: Cyperus teres has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help in treating wounds, infections, and skin irritations. In Ayurvedic medicine, Cyperus teres is used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, cough, and digestive problems. It is also believed to have antiseptic and antibacterial properties, making it effective in treating respiratory infections. Additionally, the plant is used in traditional medicine to treat eye and ear problems.

These constituents have been reported to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.
